mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of 2020-01-15 indicates easing cycle underway
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following rate cuts
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend indicates robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,383k as of 2020-01-15 reflects continued employment growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with month-over-month PAYEMS growth (correlation < 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and signals recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen and earnings forecasts are cut.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 527,994M as of 2020-01-15 shows retail demand levels
regime_hint: early-stage demand deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a 2%+ RSAFS decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% year-over-year indicates strong manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization, signaling healthy corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.677 as of 2020-01-15 reflects current production levels
regime_hint: expansion phase with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates (correlation < 0.12), or mechanism fails to predict positive returns in 50%+ of high-growth INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises 0.3 percentage points or more in a single month while PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% and PAYEMS at 152,383k as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where both UNRATE rises 0.3pp and PAYEMS declines, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth indicates broad-based consumer and construction demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as sentiment improves.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises month-over-month AND RSAFS rises month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,365k and RSAFS at 527,994M as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and retail sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS rise month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS remains above 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 258.501 and FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.0% annualized indicates sustained economic expansion and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are raised in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate exceeds 2.0% in the most recent quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,121.112B as of 2020-01-15 reflects current economic output
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (correlation < 0.18), or mechanism fails to predict positive returns in 55%+ of periods with GDPC1 growth > 2.0%.
